clusters.title: House Committee Subpoenas Pam Bondi Over Epstein Files
clusters.summary: The House Oversight Committee has voted to subpoena Attorney General Pam Bondi regarding her handling of files related to the Epstein case. This decision was made despite opposition from the committee's Republican chairman, with some GOP members joining Democrats in support of the subpoena. The move underscores ongoing scrutiny over the Epstein investigation and the actions of various officials involved.

clusters.title: Senate Republicans Reject Measure to Limit Iran Military Actions
clusters.summary: The U.S. Senate voted down a proposal aimed at restricting military action against Iran, marking the first congressional vote on the ongoing conflict. The legislation, which sought to require congressional approval for any military engagement, faced opposition primarily from Republican senators. This vote reflects the party's support for the current administration's approach to Iran and highlights the ongoing debate over war powers in Congress.

clusters.title: Cuba Faces Widespread Blackout Amid Energy Crisis
clusters.summary: A significant power outage struck Cuba, affecting two-thirds of the island, including the capital, Havana. This blackout comes as the country grapples with dwindling oil reserves and increasing external pressures, highlighting ongoing energy challenges. Millions of residents were left without electricity, exacerbating the difficulties faced by the population.

items.title: Cuba hit by massive power outage amid US oil pressure
items.description: A massive blackout left two-thirds of Cuba, including its capital Havana, without electricity.

items.title: Blackout in Cuba leaves millions without power amid US oil chokehold
items.description:
Latest outage darkens island facing dwindling oil reserves and increasing pressure from Washington

A blackout hit the western half of Cuba on Wednesday, leaving millions of people in Havana and beyond without power in the latest outage to affect an island struggling with dwindling oil reserves and a crumbling electricity grid.

The government’s Electric Union confirmed the outage on social platform X, saying it affected people from the western city of Pinar del Rio to the central town of Camaguey.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Investigation Reveals Widespread Abuse by Rhode Island Priests
clusters.summary: An extensive investigation has uncovered that approximately 75 priests in the Catholic Diocese of Providence, Rhode Island, have been implicated in the sexual abuse of over 300 children since 1950. This revelation highlights a long history of abuse within the diocese, prompting calls for accountability and reform. The findings have raised significant concerns about the church's handling of abuse allegations and the protection of vulnerable individuals.

items.title: What to know about the investigation into Catholic priests in Rhode Island and sexual abuse charges
items.description: A new investigation into the Catholic Diocese of Providence, Rhode Island shows that an estimated 75 priests abused more than 300 children since 1950

items.title: Sprawling investigation finds decades of sexual abuse among Catholic priests in Rhode Island - AP News
items.description: Sprawling investigation finds decades of sexual abuse among Catholic priests in Rhode Island  AP News

clusters.title: Senate Rejects Measure to Limit Military Action Against Iran
clusters.summary: The Senate has voted against a measure aimed at limiting U.S. military operations in Iran, maintaining the current level of military engagement. The decision reflects ongoing debates about the extent of executive power in military matters and the U.S. role in the region. This rejection underscores the complexities surrounding U.S. foreign policy and military strategy in the Middle East.

items.description: Senate rejects war powers measure to withdraw forces from Iran  Los Angeles Times

clusters.title: Judge Orders Refunds for Companies After Supreme Court Ruling
clusters.summary: A federal judge in New York has ruled that companies are entitled to refunds for tariffs that were recently overturned by the Supreme Court. This decision marks a significant setback for the Trump administration's tariff policies, impacting businesses that had paid these tariffs. The ruling highlights ongoing legal disputes surrounding trade practices and their financial implications for affected companies.

items.title: Judge rules companies are entitled to refunds for Trump tariffs overturned by the Supreme Court - AP News
items.description: Judge rules companies are entitled to refunds for Trump tariffs overturned by the Supreme Court  AP News

items.description: In a defeat for the Trump administration, a federal judge in New York ruled Wednesday that companies that paid tariffs struck down last month by Supreme Court are due refunds

clusters.title: Noem Denies Relationship with Lewandowski, Calls Claims 'Tabloid Garbage'
clusters.summary: South Dakota Governor Kristi Noem faced questions regarding her reported romantic relationship with Corey Lewandowski, a former Trump campaign manager. In response, she dismissed the inquiries as 'tabloid garbage,' emphasizing her disapproval of the speculation surrounding her personal life. The exchange highlights ongoing scrutiny of political figures' private relationships amidst a charged political climate.

clusters.title: House Ethics Probe Launched into Rep. Tony Gonzales
clusters.summary: The House Ethics Committee has initiated an investigation into Representative Tony Gonzales following allegations of inappropriate conduct involving a staff member. The probe reportedly finds substantial reason to believe that Gonzales engaged in a sexual relationship with the staffer, raising concerns about potential violations of House rules. This investigation underscores ongoing scrutiny of ethical standards among lawmakers.

clusters.title: Nvidia CEO Huang Discusses Future of OpenAI Investments
clusters.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has stated that the company's recent $30 billion investment in OpenAI could be its final major funding round. He ruled out a potential $100 billion investment, emphasizing the need for sustainable growth and prudent financial strategies in the tech sector. This statement highlights Nvidia's cautious approach to further financial commitments in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

clusters.title: Apple Unveils $599 MacBook Neo to Compete in PC Market
clusters.summary: Apple has introduced the MacBook Neo, priced at $599, aiming to compete with both Chromebooks and Windows PCs. This new model features upgraded specifications designed to attract a wider range of consumers. The launch is seen as a strategic move to strengthen Apple's position in the increasingly competitive laptop market.

clusters.title: Putin Considers Redirecting Gas Supplies Away from Europe
clusters.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin announced that the country may redirect its gas supplies away from Europe, citing a surge in energy production from Iran as a contributing factor. This potential shift could significantly impact European energy markets, which are already facing challenges due to geopolitical tensions. The decision reflects ongoing concerns about energy security and supply stability in the region.

clusters.title: Rams Acquire All-Pro Cornerback Trent McDuffie from Chiefs
clusters.summary: The Los Angeles Rams are finalizing a trade to acquire All-Pro cornerback Trent McDuffie from the Kansas City Chiefs. This move is significant for both teams, as the Rams bolster their defense while the Chiefs look to reshape their roster. The trade has sparked discussions among analysts regarding its implications for both franchises moving forward.

clusters.title: US Kills Iranian Leader Linked to Trump Assassination Plot
clusters.summary: The United States has confirmed the death of an Iranian leader implicated in a plot to assassinate former President Donald Trump. This operation is seen as a significant action in U.S.-Iran relations and has sparked discussions about its implications for regional stability and security. The event highlights ongoing tensions between the two nations and the complexities of international diplomacy.

clusters.title: Global 15% Tariff Set to Begin This Week Amid Trump Duties Shift
clusters.summary: A global tariff increase to 15% is scheduled to take effect this week, as announced by financial analyst Bessent. The move is expected to impact international trade dynamics, with predictions that tariffs imposed by the Trump administration may revert to previous levels later this year. This development highlights ongoing tensions in trade relations and the potential for economic adjustments.

clusters.title: Crockett Concedes Texas Senate Race to Talarico
clusters.summary: In a closely watched Texas Senate race, candidate Crockett has conceded to Talarico, acknowledging the shifting political landscape in the state. Crockett expressed concerns about voter disenfranchisement during the campaign, while Talarico's victory is seen as a sign of potential Democratic gains in Texas. This election outcome highlights ongoing debates about electoral access and representation in the region.

clusters.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Emerges as Frontrunner for Iran's Leadership
clusters.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of current Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, is being positioned as a potential successor to his father amid speculation about the future of Iran's leadership. His candidacy is significant as it may influence the direction of the Islamic Republic and its policies, especially in light of ongoing domestic and international challenges. The discussion around his possible ascension reflects broader concerns about stability and governance in Iran.

clusters.title: Texas GOP Primaries Set for May Runoffs in Senate and AG Races
clusters.summary: Texas will see runoff elections in both the GOP Senate and attorney general primaries this May. Candidates from the Republican Party, including Roy and Middleton for attorney general, are vying for their party's nomination, while Democrats are rallying behind Talarico in the Senate race. These primaries are significant as they will shape the political landscape ahead of the general elections.

clusters.title: Six U.S. Service Members Killed in Iran Military Operation
clusters.summary: The U.S. government has confirmed the deaths of six American service members during military operations in Iran that began with coordinated strikes by U.S. and Israeli forces. Former President Trump indicated that the strikes could continue for weeks and did not dismiss the possibility of deploying ground troops if deemed necessary. This escalation in military action underscores rising tensions in the region and the potential for further conflict.

items.title: What we know about U.S. service members killed in the Iran war
items.description: The U.S. government has said six U.S. service members have been killed in the military operation that started early Saturday with U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran.

items.title: Trump says strikes on Iran could take weeks as U.S. urges Americans to leave the region
items.description: Six American service members have been killed. Trump doesn’t rule out ground troops to Iran if ‘necessary.’

clusters.title: Commerce Secretary Lutnick to Testify on Epstein Connections
clusters.summary: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick has agreed to testify before the House Oversight Committee regarding his connections to Jeffrey Epstein. clusters.title: US Closes Embassies and Urges Evacuations in the Middle East
clusters.summary: The United States has ordered the closure of several embassies in the Middle East and is urging American citizens to evacuate the region. This decision comes amid escalating tensions and security concerns, prompting the government to halt operations at three key diplomatic missions. The move underscores the urgency of ensuring the safety of American personnel and citizens abroad.

clusters.title: Iran War Forces Travelers to Evacuate Amid Safety Concerns
clusters.summary: The ongoing conflict in Iran has left hundreds of thousands of travelers stranded across the Middle East, prompting urgent calls for evacuation. The U.S. government has advised its citizens to leave 14 countries in the region due to escalating safety risks. This situation highlights the broader implications of the conflict on international travel and security.

clusters.title: Trump Claims Role in Israel's Decision on Iran Strikes
clusters.summary: Former President Donald Trump stated that he played a significant role in influencing Israel's decision to conduct strikes against Iran, asserting that he 'forced' their hand in the matter. This claim has sparked debate, particularly in light of contrasting accounts from other political figures, including Senator Marco Rubio. The implications of Trump's statement touch on U.S.-Israel relations and the ongoing tensions surrounding Iran's nuclear program.

clusters.summary: Drone strikes attributed to Iranian forces targeted Amazon data centers in the UAE, leading to significant disruptions in banking and payment services. The incident underscores the vulnerabilities of critical infrastructure to physical attacks, raising concerns about the security of digital operations in the region. This event highlights the intersection of geopolitical tensions and technological reliance in the modern economy.
